questions.q: In which century did the astronomer-polymath Varahamihira live and work?
questions.explanation: Varahamihira flourished in the 6th century CE, working largely around the city of Ujjain.

questions.q: Varahamihira is most strongly associated with which ancient Indian city, a major center of astronomy?
questions.explanation: Varahamihira is celebrated as the polymath of Ujjain, a renowned center of astronomy in ancient India.

questions.q: Which of these subjects was a major focus of Varahamihira's work?
questions.explanation: Varahamihira is best known for compiling astronomy and astrology alongside other natural sciences.

questions.q: The Pancha-Siddhantika by Varahamihira is a treatise on which subject?
questions.explanation: The Pancha-Siddhantika is Varahamihira's astronomical treatise summarizing five earlier astronomical schools.

questions.q: The title 'Pancha-Siddhantika' refers to a summary of how many earlier astronomical treatises (siddhantas)?
questions.explanation: 'Pancha' means five, and the work summarizes five earlier siddhantas (astronomical canons).

questions.q: Which encyclopedic work by Varahamihira covers astronomy, astrology and natural science?
questions.explanation: The Brihat Samhita is Varahamihira's wide-ranging compendium covering astronomy, astrology and many natural phenomena.

questions.q: Varahamihira is described as a 'polymath.' What does that term mean?
questions.explanation: A polymath is someone with expertise spanning many fields, which fits Varahamihira's broad output.

questions.q: In which broad region did Varahamihira live?
questions.explanation: Varahamihira was an Indian astronomer who worked in the region of Ujjain on the Indian subcontinent.

questions.q: The Brihat Samhita is best categorized as which kind of text?
options: An encyclopedic compendium of many topics
questions.explanation: The Brihat Samhita is an encyclopedic work touching on astronomy, weather, agriculture, gemology and much more.

questions.q: Varahamihira's works are written primarily in which language?
questions.explanation: Like most classical Indian scientific texts of his era, Varahamihira wrote in Sanskrit.

questions.q: The Pancha-Siddhantika notably preserves a version of which influential astronomical text?
questions.explanation: The Pancha-Siddhantika preserves a summary of the older Surya Siddhanta among the five siddhantas it covers.

questions.q: One of the five siddhantas summarized by Varahamihira shows strong influence from which foreign tradition?
questions.explanation: The Romaka and Paulisa siddhantas reflect Greek/Hellenistic astronomical influence transmitted to India.

questions.q: Varahamihira is traditionally counted among the Navaratnas (Nine Gems) said to be at whose court?
questions.explanation: Tradition places Varahamihira among the nine gems associated with the legendary court of Vikramaditya.

questions.q: Besides astronomy, the Brihat Samhita famously includes a chapter on which practical subject?
questions.explanation: The Brihat Samhita includes a well-known treatment of dakargala, the science of locating underground water sources.

questions.q: Varahamihira's work on the science of gemstones and pearls falls under which broad field covered in the Brihat Samhita?
questions.explanation: The Brihat Samhita contains material on gems and pearls, part of the gemological knowledge (ratna-shastra) of the era.

questions.q: Varahamihira lived shortly after which earlier great Indian astronomer-mathematician, whose ideas influenced him?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata (born 476 CE) preceded Varahamihira, and his astronomy influenced the 6th-century traditions Varahamihira compiled.

questions.q: The Brihat Jataka, attributed to Varahamihira, is a foundational text in which field?
questions.explanation: The Brihat Jataka is a classic text on horoscopic astrology within the jyotisha tradition.

questions.q: Within Indian astronomical tradition, jyotisha originally encompassed which combination of disciplines?
options: Astronomy together with astrology and calendar science
questions.explanation: Jyotisha covered mathematical astronomy, calendrical computation and astrology, all of which Varahamihira addressed.

questions.q: Varahamihira's contributions in the Pancha-Siddhantika include work on which mathematical tool central to astronomy?
questions.explanation: The Pancha-Siddhantika contains rules and values for sines, essential for computing planetary positions.

questions.q: Why is the Pancha-Siddhantika especially valuable to historians of science?
options: It preserves the content of older astronomical texts that are otherwise lost
It contains the first map of the Americas
questions.explanation: By summarizing five earlier siddhantas, Varahamihira's work preserves knowledge of texts that have not survived independently.

questions.q: The 'sun' emblem often associated with Varahamihira reflects his deep engagement with which body of knowledge?
questions.explanation: His astronomical work centered on the heavens, including solar and planetary motions, fitting a solar emblem.

questions.q: Varahamihira's name 'Varaha' is connected by tradition to which symbolic association?
options: A reference to the boar avatar / a title
questions.explanation: Tradition links 'Varaha' (boar) to legends about him, and the name became part of his honored identity.

questions.q: The five siddhantas summarized in the Pancha-Siddhantika include the Paulisa, Romaka, Vasishtha, Saura, and which fifth?
questions.explanation: The five siddhantas are the Paulisa, Romaka, Vasishtha, Saura (Surya), and Paitamaha (Brahma) Siddhantas.

questions.q: The name 'Romaka Siddhanta,' one of Varahamihira's five canons, derives from a word indicating influence from where?
questions.explanation: 'Romaka' refers to Rome, signaling Greco-Roman (Hellenistic) astronomical influence on that siddhanta.

questions.q: Varahamihira's death is traditionally dated to approximately which year?
questions.explanation: Varahamihira is traditionally said to have died around 587 CE, consistent with his 6th-century activity.

questions.q: In the Pancha-Siddhantika, Varahamihira used which calendrical era as a reference epoch for his calculations?
questions.explanation: Varahamihira's astronomical computations in the Pancha-Siddhantika are referenced to the Saka era.

questions.q: Varahamihira's mathematical work includes an early treatment of which combinatorial-style construction?
options: A version of the binomial-style triangle of coefficients
questions.explanation: Varahamihira discussed combinatorial computations related to the array of binomial coefficients (the 'Meru' / Pascal-type triangle).

questions.q: The Brihat Samhita's breadth means it is often cited by historians as a source on Gupta-era which of the following?
options: Social customs, architecture and natural phenomena
Transoceanic trade routes to the Americas
questions.explanation: Because it surveys so many topics, the Brihat Samhita is a key window into Gupta-era customs, architecture and natural science.

questions.q: Varahamihira's astrological writings helped systematize which branch dealing with the casting of birth charts?
questions.explanation: His Brihat Jataka systematized hora (jataka), the natal astrology of casting and interpreting birth horoscopes.

questions.q: Which statement best captures Varahamihira's lasting significance in the history of science?
options: He compiled and transmitted astronomical, astrological and natural-science knowledge of his age
He discovered the circulation of blood
questions.explanation: Varahamihira's enduring importance lies in compiling and preserving the astronomy, astrology and natural science of 6th-century India.
